message:
feat: Configurable allocator + fair benchmark memory limits

Two changes for better resource management:

1. Configurable allocator via .edgebox.json:
   - "allocator": "system" (default) - libc malloc, reclaims freed memory
   - "allocator": "bump" - O(1) alloc, for serverless <=15min workloads
   - Fixes OOM in sandbox/test262 by using system allocator by default

2. Fair benchmark comparison - all runtimes now have same 2GB heap limit:
   - EdgeBox: heap_size default changed from 256MB to 2GB
   - EdgeBox daemon: heap_size changed from 16MB to 2GB
   - Node.js: NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=2048"
   - Bun: BUN_JSC_forceRAMSize=2147483648

This ensures apples-to-apples comparison in CI benchmarks.

files.patch:
@@ -76,6 +76,13 @@ EDGEBOXD="$ROOT_DIR/zig-out/bin/edgeboxd"
 # Suppress debug/info messages during benchmarks
 export EDGEBOX_QUIET=1
 
+# Set same memory limit (2 GB) for all runtimes - fair apples-to-apples comparison
+# EdgeBox: configured via heap_size in .edgebox.json or source defaults (2 GB)
+# Node.js: max old space size in MB
+export NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=2048"
+# Bun: JSC RAM size in bytes (2 GB)
+export BUN_JSC_forceRAMSize=2147483648
+
 # Detect platform
 PLATFORM=$(uname -s)
 ARCH=$(uname -m)
